bird.name: Puerto Rican bullfinch
bird.summary: The Puerto Rican bullfinch (Melopyrrha portoricensis) is a small bullfinch tanager endemic to the archipelago of Puerto Rico. The species c…

relations.subject: Puerto Rican bullfinch
relations.evidence: Bullfinches are believed to be most common in dense mountain forests but can also be found in lower forests with dense undergrowth, coffee plantations, thick brushy areas, dry coastal thickets, and rarely in mangroves.{{cite book|author=Raffaele, H.A
